summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM context, exfiltrates to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@1.0.1 is a brand-impersonating npm package (claims to be 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', repository points to a non-existent github.com/anthropic-toolkit org) whose scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests developer/CI identity from the installer's machine, then POSTs the payload as JSON to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include: os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, OS platform/version, current working directory, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and repo.git/config, the last 50 committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, GitHub CLI login/email from hosts.yml, comments (typically emails) from all ~/.ssh/*.pub files, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, ~/.config/gcloud/properties (gcpAccount, gcpProject), and ~/.aws/config profile names. The package frames the behavior as 'anonymous diagnostics' with an ANTHROPIC_TOOLKIT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED opt-out, but the transmitted payload is explicitly identifying — a targeted reconnaissance profile of each developer/CI environment tied to its SCM identity, SSH key fingerprints, and cloud account names. Brand impersonation of Anthropic combined with install-time exfiltration to an attacker-controlled endpoint matches the typosquat-with-payload active-attack pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
